chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 118 – Arrival of Abu Ja’far Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف بسنديه بعبدال بن أحم د - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/15)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف بسنديه بعبدال بن أحم د - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/15)
text: إِنَّ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ كَانَ آخِرَ مَنْ بَقِيَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ وَ كَانَ رَجُلًا مُنْقَطِعاً إِلَيْنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَ كَانَ يَقْعُدُ فِي مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ هُوَ مُعْتَجِرٌ بِعِمَامَةٍ سَوْدَاءَ وَ كَانَ يُنَادِي يَا بَاقِرَ الْعِلْمِ يَا بَاقِرَ الْعِلْمِ

فَكَانَ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ يَقُولُونَ جَابِرٌ يَهْجُرُ فَكَانَ يَقُولُ لَا وَ اللَّهِ مَا أَهْجُرُ وَ لَكِنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَقُولُ إِنَّكَ سَتُدْرِكُ رَجُلًا مِنِّي اسْمُهُ اسْمِي وَ شَمَائِلُهُ شَمَائِلِي يَبْقُرُ الْعِلْمَ بَقْراً فَذَاكَ الَّذِي دَعَانِي إِلَى مَا أَقُولُ

قَالَ فَبَيْنَا جَابِرٌ يَتَرَدَّدُ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ فِي بَعْضِ طُرُقِ الْمَدِينَةِ إِذْ مَرَّ بِطَرِيقٍ فِي ذَاكَ الطَّرِيقِ كُتَّابٌ فِيهِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ فَلَمَّا نَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ قَالَ يَا غُلَامُ أَقْبِلْ فَأَقْبَلَ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ أَدْبِرْ فَأَدْبَرَ ثُمَّ قَالَ شَمَائِلُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ الَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ يَا غُلَامُ مَا اسْمُكَ قَالَ اسْمِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ فَأَقْبَلَ عَلَيْهِ يُقَبِّلُ رَأْسَهُ وَ يَقُولُ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي أَبُوكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يُقْرِئُكَ السَّلَامَ وَ يَقُولُ ذَلِكَ

قَالَ فَرَجَعَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ إِلَى أَبِيهِ وَ هُوَ ذَعِرٌ فَأَخْبَرَهُ الْخَبَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا بُنَيَّ وَ قَدْ فَعَلَهَا جَابِرٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ الْزَمْ بَيْتَكَ يَا بُنَيَّ فَكَانَ جَابِرٌ يَأْتِيهِ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَ كَانَ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ يَقُولُونَ وَا عَجَبَاهْ لِجَابِرٍ يَأْتِي هَذَا الْغُلَامَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَ هُوَ آخِرُ مَنْ بَقِيَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله )

فَلَمْ يَلْبَثْ أَنْ مَضَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَكَانَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ يَأْتِيهِ عَلَى وَجْهِ الْكَرَامَةِ لِصُحْبَتِهِ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ فَجَلَسَ ( عليه السلام ) يُحَدِّثُهُمْ عَنِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فَقَالَ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ مَا رَأَيْنَا أَحَداً أَجْرَأَ مِنْ هَذَا

فَلَمَّا رَأَى مَا يَقُولُونَ حَدَّثَهُمْ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ مَا رَأَيْنَا أَحَداً قَطُّ أَكْذَبَ مِنْ هَذَا يُحَدِّثُنَا عَمَّنْ لَمْ يَرَهُ فَلَمَّا رَأَى مَا يَقُولُونَ حَدَّثَهُمْ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَصَدَّقُوهُ وَ كَانَ جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَأْتِيهِ فَيَتَعَلَّمُ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Aban Bin Taghlub,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Jabir Bin Abdullah Al-Ansary was the last ones to remain, from the companions of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and he was a man who had cut himself off to us<sup>asws</sup>, the People<sup>asws</sup> of the Household, and he was sitting in the Masjid of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and he was turbaned with a black turban, and he was calling out, ‘يَا بَاقِرَ الْعِلْمِ يَا بَاقِرَ الْعِلْم’ ‘O expounder of the knowledge! O expounder of the knowledge!

And it was so that the people of Al-Medina were saying, ‘Jabir is hallucinating’. So he was saying, ‘No, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I am not hallucinating. But, I heard Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> saying: ‘You (Jabir) would be coming across a man from me<sup>saww</sup>, his<sup>asws</sup> name would be my<sup>saww</sup> name, and his<sup>asws</sup> mannerisms would be my<sup>saww</sup> mannerisms. He<sup>asws</sup> would expound the knowledge with an expansion’. So that is what is calling me to what I am saying’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So while Jabir was walking around one day in one of the streets of Al-Median, when some scribes passed by in that street, among whom was Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>. So when he looked at him<sup>asws</sup>, he said, ‘O boy<sup>asws</sup>! Come!’ So, he<sup>asws</sup> came. Then he said, ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! The mannerisms of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, by the One<sup>azwj</sup> in Whose Hand is my soul! O boy<sup>asws</sup>! What is your<sup>asws</sup> name?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> name is Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>’. So he went faced him<sup>asws</sup>, and kissed his<sup>asws</sup> head and he was saying, ‘By my father and my mother be (sacrificed) for you<sup>asws</sup>! Your<sup>asws</sup> father Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> conveys the greetings to you<sup>asws</sup> and he<sup>saww</sup> was saying (all) that’.

Heasws said: ‘So Muhammadasws Bin Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws returned to hisasws fatherasws, and heasws was alarmed, So, heasws informed himasws of the news. So, heasws said to himasws: ‘O myasws sonasws, and Jabir did it?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes’. Heasws said: ‘Necessitated yourasws house (stay at home), O myasws son!’ So Jabir used to come to himasws at both ends of the day, and the people of Al-Medina were saying, ‘O how strange of Jabir to come to this boyasws at both ends of the day, and he is the last one to remain from the companions of Rasool-Allahsaww’.

It was not long before Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> passed away (martyred), and Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> used to go to him (Jabir Bin Abdullah) upon the aspect of the honour of his companionship to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. He<sup>asws</sup> would sit and narrate to them (directly) from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High. The people of Al-Medina would say, ‘We have not seen anyone so audacious than this one’.

When he<sup>asws</sup> saw what they were saying, he<sup>asws</sup> narrated to them (directly) from Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. So, the people of Al-Medina said, ‘We have not seen anyone at all more lying than this one (Nouzobillah), narrating to us from the one<sup>saww</sup> he<sup>asws</sup> has not even seen. When he<sup>asws</sup> saw what they were saying, he<sup>asws</sup> narrated to them from Jabir Bin Abdullah. (In that way) they ratified him<sup>asws</sup>, and it was so that Jabir Bin Abdullah used to come to him<sup>asws</sup>, to learn from him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: Muhammad ibn al-Hassan has narrated from ‘Abdallah ibn Ahmad has narrated a similar hadith.
